The provided retrieval does not contain an explicit reference to Weerheijm et al’s work; however, observed literature reports MIH prevalence among permanent molars and incisors spanning 1.6% to 36.5%. Now review Agent1’s core claims:

Therefore, distilled into one clear factual sentence each:

Second sentence explains why Option C stands out medically despite being broader — because it uniquely captures known variation seen globally due to population differences.

Thus refined version meeting all constraints:

The prevalence of MIH in permanent molars and incisors varies widely, ranging from 1.6% to 36.5%, reflecting substantial inter-population heterogeneity in affected individuals. Because this range falls completely within option C (6.2%–48.8%), clinicians can reasonably expect encounter rates consistent with that distribution when assessing pediatric oral health patterns.
